summary: Matched the stage-related item source src/melee/it/items/it_2E6A.c, identified in comments as ityaku.c, and flipped it from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The fix centered on replacing placeholder integer/void callback plumbing with typed Item_GObj/Ground callback signatures, unifying the it_2E6A item variable layout with a union for JObj-vs-Vec3 storage, correcting ItemAttr size/initializer assumptions, and propagating the refined types into grmaterial, grcorneria, grticeclimber, and ground vars.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/it_2E6A.c is built as Matching instead of NonMatching.

Cleaned up it_2E6A.c by removing commented-out nonmatching scaffolding and using direct typed calls, local declarations, NULL checks, GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ usage, and a normal while(true) trap after the ityaku.c OSReport path.

Changed it_802E6AEC to take typed callback function pointers: init-style callback Item_GObj*/Ground*, pickup/touch-position callback Item_GObj*/Ground*/Vec3*/HSD_GObj*/f32, and toucher callback Item_GObj*/Ground*/HSD_GObj*.
Updated it_802E6F7C, it_802E6FC0, and it_802E7054 to call the typed callback fields directly with the stored Ground pointer rather than casting void pointers or HSD_GObj-only callbacks.
Merged the previous two it_2E6A item-var structs into one it_2E6A_ItemVars_1 using a union at offset +4 for either HSD_JObj* x4 or Vec3 x4_vec, with x2 documenting the union tag: 1=jobj, 2=vec.
Typed it_2E6A item variables x10/x14/x18/x1C as Ground* and exact callback pointer types instead of void* fields.
Removed it_2E6A_ItemVars_2 from Item's xDD4_itemVar union and deleted extra ItemAttr fields x84 through x9C from src/melee/it/types.h.
Corrected the it_803F8C08 ItemAttr initializer in it_2E6A.c so destroy_gfx and the following x68-style field are -1 instead of 0, matching the 0xFFFFFFFF comments.
Changed grMaterial_801C8CFC and grMaterial_801C8D44 to return Item_GObj* from it_802E6AEC and to accept the same typed callback pointers.
Changed grMaterial_801C8DE0 and grMaterial_801C8E08 to accept Item_GObj* instead of generic HSD_GObj*/int-like placeholders.

Updated Corneria and Icicle Mountain callback prototypes to the new item/ground callback shapes, including fn_801E12D0(Item_GObj*, Ground*) and grTIceClimber_80221208(Item_GObj*, Ground*, Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, f32).
Changed grCorneria_GroundVars fields x120 and x124 from u32 to Item_GObj*, indicating the stage ground vars hold spawned item object references.
Added forward includes for item and ground types where needed instead of relying on ad hoc extern declarations.
smart_moves: The match was achieved by improving type information across call boundaries instead of preserving casts and placeholder signatures; this made the generated code closer to the original calling convention.
Using a union for it_2E6A_ItemVars_1 captured the actual overlapping storage at item var offset +4, avoiding duplicated structs that represented alternate interpretations of the same memory.
Returning Item_GObj* from grMaterial_801C8CFC/grMaterial_801C8D44 matched their callers, especially grTIceClimber_80221288, where the return is immediately checked and then passed to item helpers.
Replacing '&pos != 0' with 'pos != 0' fixed a clear pointer-test artifact in it_802E6AEC and made the Vec3-spawn path meaningful.
Propagating Item_GObj* through grmaterial and stage callback prototypes reduced generic HSD_GObj and integer casts while keeping the evidence tied to actual users of it_802E6AEC.
Correcting ItemAttr structure size and the -1 initializer values removed misleading padding/field assumptions that affected the item attribute literal.
decomp_lessons: When an item file only matches with ugly casts, inspect its stage wrapper functions; typed callback signatures in the ground/stage layer can be the missing constraint.
Item xDD4 unions often represent overlapping runtime modes, not distinct structs that both need to live in the main Item union. A tagged union can be more accurate and more match-friendly.
If a helper function wraps a spawn routine and callers test the result, prefer the concrete object pointer return type over bool/int placeholders.
Do not add speculative trailing struct fields just because an initializer comment says fields might be missing; verify structure size against real initializers and users.
Small initializer constants matter for matching: 0 versus -1 is also 0x00000000 versus 0xFFFFFFFF and can affect data matching.
Forward headers should be updated when replacing placeholder types with real cross-system types; this keeps prototypes accurate without creating broad includes.
A callback stored in item vars may need contextual data from the item var block itself, such as Ground*, not just the triggering gobj.
naming_conventions: The PR title identifies it_2E6A as ityaku; the file still uses address-based symbol names such as it_802E6AEC and it_802E6D60.
Callback arguments in updated headers are named by position in grMaterial prototypes, e.g. arg4, arg5, arg6, but their function pointer types are explicit.
The it_2E6A item-var union names preserve offset-based names, with x4 for HSD_JObj* and x4_vec for the Vec3 overlay.
Stage functions retain existing generated names such as fn_801E12D0 and grTIceClimber_80221208 while gaining concrete Item_GObj/Ground parameter types.
The grTIceClimber capitalization pattern remains as existing project style, including grTIceClimber_80221208.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed object status from NonMatching to Matching only after reducing casts and placeholder parameters in the source and dependent headers.
Used exact callback pointer signatures to let indirect calls compile into the expected argument setup rather than casting at each callsite.
Used a const Vec3 scale literal and local assignment for HSD_JObjSetScale, likely matching the original rodata/data placement better than a mutable static.
Replaced a manual goto self-loop with while(true), preserving the infinite trap behavior after OSReport while presenting cleaner C.
Removed PAD_STACK(16) from grTIceClimber_80221208 after the corrected five-argument callback signature made the stack layout match without padding.
Changed bool-style result handling to Item_GObj* result_1 != NULL in grTIceClimber_80221288, aligning the wrapper return type with object-pointer use.
Removed casts such as (HSD_GObj*) item_gobj in several places where Item_GObj* is already compatible with the expected typed helper.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only recorded discussion count is one issue comment, but no human text excerpt was provided, so reviewer intent cannot be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename it_2E6A or ityaku symbols to semantic names.
Search for other item var structs with duplicated JObj-versus-Vec3 layouts that could be converted to tagged unions.
Audit grMaterial_801C8CFC and grMaterial_801C8D44 callers for remaining placeholder callback types.
Check whether ItemAttr field removal affected other common item attribute initializers or required follow-up struct documentation.
Look for additional stage-related item files still marked NonMatching near itoctarock, it_2E5A, itarwinglaser, itoctarockstone, and itleadead.
